# UC-13.3.12 · Prometheus TSDB Compaction Failures and Storage Health

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Fault, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Prometheus stores mountains of tiny measurement slips on disk like a librarian merging folders so old papers stay tidy. When that merging jams because the disk is full, files are damaged, or the pile of labels explodes, graphs can go spotty and alarms may hush; Splunk spots those jams early and points engineers to the right shelf.*

---

## Description

Detects Prometheus time-series database compaction and on-disk health regressions by correlating self-monitoring counters and histograms with structured TSDB status JSON, server logs, and host Performance context. The TSDB keeps an in-memory head block fed by a write-ahead log, then cuts immutable blocks, runs compaction to merge overlapping ranges, and prunes by time and size retention while memory-mapping chunk files. Failures surface when free space disappears, fsync returns errors, on-disk blocks are damaged, cardinality explodes in the head, or compaction runs out of memory. Customer impact includes stair-step gaps in Grafana curves, recording rules that evaluate against partial data, alert rules that go quiet because `ALERTS` series stall, orphaned blocks that confuse operators, and restart loops where WAL replay cannot converge. This control is intentionally narrower than UC-13.3.1 legacy poller ingestion, UC-13.3.2 general Prometheus metric path fidelity, UC-13.3.3 paging integrations, UC-13.3.4 coverage gap catalogs, UC-13.3.5 alert storms, UC-13.3.7 trace sampling, UC-13.3.11 scrape and discovery failures, UC-13.3.14 OpenTelemetry exporter drops, and UC-13.3.15 collector throughput economics.

## Value

Storage and observability owners gain a single Splunk narrative that connects rising `prometheus_tsdb_head_series`, stalled `prometheus_tsdb_compactions_total` progress, non-zero `prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total`, WAL corruption counters, reload failures, TSDB status head stats, and disk pressure before executives see unexplained blank charts. Finance and capacity reviewers receive defensible evidence that retention-by-size and head cardinality policies actually match disk budgets, while incident teams receive prioritized rows that distinguish maintenance windows from true storage engine faults. The saved search preserves forensic joins to inventory ownership and scheduled change slots so post-incident reviews do not depend on chat history alone.

## Implementation

Scrape Prometheus self-`/metrics` into `index=prometheus_metrics`, ship `/api/v1/status/tsdb` and optional `/api/v1/status/flags` JSON to `index=prometheus` with dedicated sourcetypes, forward stderr or journald logs to `prometheus:log`, accelerate CIM Performance for Prometheus hosts, maintain `prometheus_tsdb_inventory.csv`, save `observability_uc_13_3_12_prometheus_tsdb_compaction_health` on a fifteen-minute cadence with `earliest=-24h@h`, route CRITICAL and HIGH rows to platform storage on-call, and archive weekly evidence exports.

## Evidence

Saved search `observability_uc_13_3_12_prometheus_tsdb_compaction_health` on a fifteen-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns on `prometheus_metrics`, `prometheus:tsdb`, and `prometheus:log`; alert routing to platform storage on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab Prometheus with a disposable `--storage.tsdb.path`, fill the filesystem to roughly ninety-five percent while ingestion continues, confirm `prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total` increases and `observability_uc_13_3_12_prometheus_tsdb_compaction_health` returns CRITICAL within fifteen minutes, then free space and verify severity decays. Optionally inject a controlled WAL anomaly on a throwaway volume and confirm MEDIUM severity when corruption counters rise while block integrity checks remain clean.

### Negative scenario

After recovery when TSDB status shows steady head chunk counts, failure counters remain flat across six hours, logs lack compaction panics, Performance disk metrics sit below policy, and maint_window covers any planned retention change, confirm severities return to LOW or INFO and the alert wrapper emits zero rows filtering to CRITICAL and HIGH only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers who operate Prometheus servers, Thanos sidecars, remote-write gateways, and the collectors that land self-metrics and TSDB admin JSON into Splunk. This use case isolates on-disk TSDB health: head block growth, WAL durability signals, compaction success, block directory integrity, retention pruning behavior, and host disk pressure that precede silent graph gaps. UC-13.3.2 remains authoritative for end-to-end Prometheus metric ingest fidelity into Splunk Metrics indexes including receiver economics; cite it when tcpin or HEC queue pressure masquerades as TSDB failure. UC-13.3.11 remains scrape-side target health, service discovery drift, and federation gaps rather than storage engine merges. UC-13.3.14 remains OpenTelemetry exporter drops of spans and metrics. UC-13.3.15 remains generalized collector throughput and memory limiter behavior. UC-13.3.4 remains fleet coverage catalogs. UC-13.3.5 remains alert storm detection. UC-13.3.7 remains trace sampling coverage. UC-13.3.1 remains legacy Nagios and Zabbix patterns. UC-13.3.3 remains PagerDuty and Opsgenie routing contracts.

Prometheus exposes operational signals both as text logs and as self-scraped metrics such as prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total, prometheus_tsdb_wal_corruptions_total, prometheus_tsdb_head_series, prometheus_tsdb_head_chunks, prometheus_tsdb_storage_blocks_bytes, prometheus_tsdb_compaction_duration_seconds, and prometheus_tsdb_reloads_failures_total. The HTTP endpoint /api/v1/status/tsdb returns JSON describing headStats, labelValueCountByLabelName, numSeries, chunkCount, timeSeriesCount, and database creation timestamps useful for capacity narratives. The /api/v1/status/flags endpoint documents effective storage retention, max bytes, and scrape flags when ingested for drift audits.

RBAC and safety: automation identities that call admin endpoints must be read-only, rate-limited, and scoped to observability networks. Never embed long-lived bearer tokens in saved searches. Legal review should confirm instance, replica, and file path fields are acceptable in log indexes. Break-glass repair commands belong in runbooks, not in Splunk alert actions.

Index design: land self-metrics in index=prometheus_metrics with OTel or TA normalization; land TSDB JSON in index=prometheus with sourcetype=prometheus:tsdb; land flags JSON with sourcetype=prometheus:flags when you need configuration receipts; land stderr or journald streams with sourcetype=prometheus:log; land host disk context via CIM Performance and optional linux:disk. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for compaction replay windows and thirty days for forensic review after storage migrations.

Risk briefing: short spikes in compaction duration after retention edits are often healthy. Single log lines during shutdown can look severe without counter deltas. Thanos uploads and snapshot agents can interleave with local compaction timelines; interpret object-store health separately.

Differentiation recap: Prometheus TSDB compaction failures, WAL corruption counters, head cardinality stress, TSDB JSON receipts, and disk pressure correlation, not generic scrape health alone, not Splunk ingest tcpin narratives, not OpenTelemetry span drops, not collector memory tuning absent TSDB signals.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch lanes plus a CSV inventory for ownership and maintenance context.

Lane one scrapes Prometheus self-metrics into index=prometheus_metrics using either the Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus receiver against http://prometheus:9090/metrics or a TA-prometheus modular input. Preserve metric_name dimensions exactly for mstats, including host, instance, cluster, and prometheus_replica labels when present. Ensure WAL and compaction counters map without accidental label drops that would hide per-replica skew.

Lane two ships Prometheus server stderr or journald units into index=prometheus with sourcetype=prometheus:log. Parse timestamps from the forwarder when container logs lack RFC3339 fields. TRUNCATE must accommodate long stack traces from Go panics during compaction.

Lane three schedules curl or wget jobs that GET /api/v1/status/tsdb every five minutes from bastion hosts or Kubernetes CronJobs, posting JSON to HTTP Event Collector with sourcetype=prometheus:tsdb. Include auth headers when admin APIs sit behind OAuth2 proxies. Optionally ingest /api/v1/status/flags on the same cadence with sourcetype=prometheus:flags for retention receipts.

Lane four accelerates Splunk CIM Performance for Linux hosts that mount TSDB volumes. Map pctDiskSpaceUsed or your deployment equivalent so tstats arms can join on host keys that match Prometheus uni_host labels.

Lane five collects optional index=os sourcetype=linux:disk rows for fullness and inode pressure that Performance summaries might omit on certain builds.

Maintain prometheus_tsdb_inventory.csv with columns host, role, retention_days, disk_capacity_gb, owner_team, scheduled_maint_window. Refresh weekly from configuration management or GitOps exports.

Example curl helpers:

```bash
curl -fsS -H 'Accept: application/json' 'http://127.0.0.1:9090/api/v1/status/tsdb' | head -c 4000
curl -fsS -H 'Accept: application/json' 'http://127.0.0.1:9090/api/v1/status/flags' | head -c 4000
curl -fsS 'http://127.0.0.1:9090/metrics' | rg 'prometheus_tsdb_(compactions|wal|head|storage|reloads)'
```

props.conf should normalize dotted JSON paths from TSDB status, handle wide metric exposition lines, and preserve authoritative _time from HEC receipt when cron skew exists.

Pre-save validation: each lane must emit rows within one hour in production; lab injections of log lines containing compaction panic substrings should appear in sourcetype=prometheus:log within two minutes.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_3_12_prometheus_tsdb_compaction_health with a fifteen-minute schedule, dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH. Throttle duplicate uni_host and cluster pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, lanes, and owner_team fragments in pager narratives.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans mstats self-metrics, prometheus:log lines, prometheus:tsdb JSON, Performance disk summaries, and linux:disk fullness so a silent lane cannot hide a genuine compaction fault. coalesce builds uni_host keys across host, instance, and cluster label drift. streamstats counts trailing failure-weighted events per host across ninety-six fifteen-minute buckets to approximate twenty-four hours. eventstats derives cluster median head series for cardinality anomaly context. case maps CRITICAL combinations of failure signals, disk fullness, and maintenance metadata into operator-facing severity. join attaches prometheus_tsdb_inventory.csv for ownership and scheduled_maint_window correlation.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.12 TSDB compaction and storage health multisearch earliest=-24h@h disk_crit_pct=85 head_growth_mult=2")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now span=15m metric_name IN ("prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total","prometheus_tsdb_wal_corruptions_total","prometheus_tsdb_head_series","prometheus_tsdb_reloads_failures_total","prometheus_tsdb_compactions_total") BY host metric_name
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,cluster,"unk"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="mstats_tsdb"
      | eval compaction_failed=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total",mv,0)
      | eval wal_corr=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_wal_corruptions_total",mv,0)
      | eval head_series_m=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_head_series",mv,0)
      | eval reload_fail=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_reloads_failures_total",mv,0)
      | stats max(compaction_failed) AS compaction_failed max(wal_corr) AS wal_corr max(head_series_m) AS head_series max(reload_fail) AS reload_fail BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval disk_used_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
    [ search index=prometheus sourcetype="prometheus:log" earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval raw=lower(_raw)
      | where match(raw,"compact|compaction|tsdb|wal|corrupt|panic|fsync|no space|mmap|reload")
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,prom_instance,prometheus_instance,fqdn,cluster,"unk"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,dc,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="prom_log"
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats count AS log_ev BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval head_series=null()
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval disk_used_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
    [ search index=prometheus sourcetype="prometheus:tsdb" earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,prometheus_instance,cluster,"unk"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="tsdb_http"
      | eval head_series=tonumber(coalesce(headStats.numSeries,numSeries,seriesCount,0))
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=tonumber(coalesce(numBlocks,blockCount,0))
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats max(head_series) AS head_series max(tsdb_num_blocks) AS tsdb_num_blocks BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval disk_used_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null() ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.pctDiskSpaceUsed) AS disk_used_pct latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS perf_host
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(perf_host)))
      | eval cluster=uni_host
      | eval lane="perf_dm"
      | eval _time=relative_time(now(),"-0h@m")
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval head_series=null()
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
    [ search index=os sourcetype=linux:disk earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="linux_disk"
      | eval pct=tonumber(coalesce(PctUseSpace,pctUsed,PercentUsedSpace,0))
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats max(pct) AS disk_used_pct BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval head_series=null()
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
| eval uni_host=lower(trim(coalesce(uni_host,host,instance,cluster)))
| stats max(compaction_failed) AS compaction_failed max(wal_corr) AS wal_corr max(head_series) AS head_series max(reload_fail) AS reload_fail sum(log_ev) AS log_ev max(disk_used_pct) AS disk_used_pct max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_used_pct max(tsdb_num_blocks) AS tsdb_num_blocks values(lane) AS lanes BY _time uni_host cluster
| sort 0 uni_host cluster + _time
| streamstats window=96 global=f current=t sum(eval(if(compaction_failed>0 OR wal_corr>0 OR log_ev>0,1,0))) AS fail_events_24h BY uni_host cluster
| eventstats median(head_series) AS cluster_median_series BY cluster
| eval disk_used_pct=tonumber(coalesce(disk_used_pct,0))
| join type=left max=0 uni_host
    [| inputlookup prometheus_tsdb_inventory.csv
     | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | fields uni_host role retention_days disk_capacity_gb owner_team scheduled_maint_window ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval role=coalesce(role,"unknown")
| eval retention_days=coalesce(retention_days,0)
| eval disk_capacity_gb=coalesce(disk_capacity_gb,0)
| eval sw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(scheduled_maint_window,""))))
| eval maint_hit=if(match(sw,"freeze|change|approved|blackout|maint"),1,0)
| eval fail_signal=if(compaction_failed>0 OR log_ev>0,1,0)
| eval head_bloat=if(isnotnull(head_series) AND isnotnull(cluster_median_series) AND cluster_median_series>500 AND head_series>(2*cluster_median_series),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    fail_signal==1 AND maint_hit==0 AND disk_used_pct>85,"CRITICAL",
    head_bloat==1,"HIGH",
    wal_corr>0,"MEDIUM",
    maint_hit==1,"LOW",
    fail_signal==1 AND disk_used_pct<=85,"HIGH",
    true(),"LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL","free_disk_space_verify_tsdb_path_promtool_tsdb_analyze_restore_snapshot",
    severity=="HIGH" AND head_bloat==1,"reduce_label_cardinality_review_scrape_configs_recording_rules",
    severity=="MEDIUM","inspect_wal_segments_restart_controlled_review_logs_for_corrupt",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_validate_scrape_and_retention_policies")
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(compaction_failed) AS compaction_failed max(wal_corr) AS wal_corr max(head_series) AS head_series max(reload_fail) AS reload_fail max(log_ev) AS log_ev max(disk_used_pct) AS disk_used_pct max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_used_pct max(tsdb_num_blocks) AS tsdb_num_blocks max(fail_events_24h) AS fail_events_24h first(cluster_median_series) AS cluster_median_series first(lanes) AS lanes first(severity) AS severity first(recommended_action) AS recommended_action first(owner_team) AS owner_team first(role) AS role first(retention_days) AS retention_days first(disk_capacity_gb) AS disk_capacity_gb first(scheduled_maint_window) AS scheduled_maint_window BY uni_host cluster
| table uni_host cluster lanes fail_events_24h cluster_median_series compaction_failed wal_corr head_series reload_fail log_ev disk_used_pct mem_used_pct tsdb_num_blocks severity recommended_action owner_team role retention_days disk_capacity_gb scheduled_maint_window last_seen
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_3_12_prometheus_tsdb_compaction_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-storage-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Prometheus TSDB $result.severity$ $result.uni_host$ $result.cluster$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_3_12_prometheus_tsdb_compaction_health | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")
```

Performance: materialize fifteen-minute summaries per uni_host into a summary index when Job Inspector queue times exceed internal objectives; widen detection windows on summaries while retaining this search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the Prometheus server: compare live /api/v1/status/tsdb JSON to the latest sourcetype=prometheus:tsdb event for the same host within one collection interval. When they diverge, verify cron skew, duplicate instance labels, and HEC token routing before blaming parsers.

Deep dive with curl /metrics on the same host: confirm prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total and prometheus_tsdb_wal_corruptions_total match mstats peaks in Splunk. For head series growth, compare PromQL rate of prometheus_tsdb_head_series against Splunk head_series fields from both mstats and TSDB JSON lanes.

Synthetic validation in lab: fill TSDB disk to roughly ninety-five percent while ingestion continues, confirm CRITICAL severity within fifteen minutes, then free space and confirm decay. For WAL-focused drills on disposable volumes, introduce controlled corruption only under written break-glass policy and confirm MEDIUM severity when wal_corr rises without block loss.

Lookup validation: confirm join hits populate owner_team and scheduled_maint_window for every production Prometheus row; empty joins indicate stale CSV paths or host normalization drift.

CIM validation: confirm Performance summaries return pctDiskSpaceUsed for Prometheus hosts; when absent, repair data model acceleration or rely on linux:disk until fixed.

Negative test: on healthy estates with quiet change windows, filtering severity to CRITICAL and HIGH should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Self-metrics missing after collector upgrade: verify prometheus receiver scrape targets, TLS SANs, and service discovery annotations; restore scrape before muting TSDB alerts.

Case B — WAL replay loop on restart: capture logs, check for partial segments, validate free space and file permissions, escalate to runbook WAL truncation steps only with backups.

Case C — Compaction failure with corrupt block errors: run promtool tsdb analyze on the block directory, quarantine damaged ULIDs, restore from object store or snapshot, restart Prometheus with validated data.

Case D — Head series explosion from a new label dimension: identify offending scrape jobs via TSDB status labelValueCountByLabelName, tighten relabel rules, split workloads, or shard Prometheus.

Case E — remote_write backlog masking local compaction: inspect queue gauges on the same host, compare leaf versus aggregator roles, avoid conflating upstream lag with TSDB merges.

Case F — Snapshot agent holding file handles: pause snapshots during compaction windows or relocate snapshot schedules per vendor guidance.

Case G — Stale block counts after retention change: allow compaction to catch up, verify flags receipts, and document expected transient series movement.

Case H — Clock skew between Prometheus and indexers: align NTP, review _time extraction, and reject false severity from mis-ordered events.

Case I — Disk full from aggressive retention size: expand volumes or reduce retention, validate filesystem quotas separately from Prometheus flags.

Case J — Thanos sidecar uploading partially compacted blocks: verify object-store permissions, sidecar readiness gates, and store-gateway consistency checks.

Case K — Kernel page cache thrashing under load: move TSDB to faster media, tune compaction concurrency cautiously, and correlate node CPU steal time.

Case L — HA failover duplicate self-metrics: dedupe by instance label, reconcile service discovery, and confirm alert routing uses stable host keys.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms covering mstats, prometheus:log, prometheus:tsdb, Performance tstats, and linux:disk; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, join, and tstats appear; final table includes analyst-visible columns for routing.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.12 TSDB compaction and storage health multisearch earliest=-24h@h disk_crit_pct=85 head_growth_mult=2")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now span=15m metric_name IN ("prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total","prometheus_tsdb_wal_corruptions_total","prometheus_tsdb_head_series","prometheus_tsdb_reloads_failures_total","prometheus_tsdb_compactions_total") BY host metric_name
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,cluster,"unk"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="mstats_tsdb"
      | eval compaction_failed=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total",mv,0)
      | eval wal_corr=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_wal_corruptions_total",mv,0)
      | eval head_series_m=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_head_series",mv,0)
      | eval reload_fail=if(metric_name=="prometheus_tsdb_reloads_failures_total",mv,0)
      | stats max(compaction_failed) AS compaction_failed max(wal_corr) AS wal_corr max(head_series_m) AS head_series max(reload_fail) AS reload_fail BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval disk_used_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
    [ search index=prometheus sourcetype="prometheus:log" earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval raw=lower(_raw)
      | where match(raw,"compact|compaction|tsdb|wal|corrupt|panic|fsync|no space|mmap|reload")
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,prom_instance,prometheus_instance,fqdn,cluster,"unk"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,dc,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="prom_log"
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats count AS log_ev BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval head_series=null()
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval disk_used_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
    [ search index=prometheus sourcetype="prometheus:tsdb" earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,prometheus_instance,cluster,"unk"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="tsdb_http"
      | eval head_series=tonumber(coalesce(headStats.numSeries,numSeries,seriesCount,0))
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=tonumber(coalesce(numBlocks,blockCount,0))
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats max(head_series) AS head_series max(tsdb_num_blocks) AS tsdb_num_blocks BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval disk_used_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null() ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.pctDiskSpaceUsed) AS disk_used_pct latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS perf_host
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(perf_host)))
      | eval cluster=uni_host
      | eval lane="perf_dm"
      | eval _time=relative_time(now(),"-0h@m")
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval head_series=null()
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
    [ search index=os sourcetype=linux:disk earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,uni_host,"unk"))))
      | eval lane="linux_disk"
      | eval pct=tonumber(coalesce(PctUseSpace,pctUsed,PercentUsedSpace,0))
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats max(pct) AS disk_used_pct BY _time uni_host cluster lane
      | eval compaction_failed=0
      | eval wal_corr=0
      | eval head_series=null()
      | eval reload_fail=0
      | eval log_ev=0
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval tsdb_num_blocks=null() ]
| eval uni_host=lower(trim(coalesce(uni_host,host,instance,cluster)))
| stats max(compaction_failed) AS compaction_failed max(wal_corr) AS wal_corr max(head_series) AS head_series max(reload_fail) AS reload_fail sum(log_ev) AS log_ev max(disk_used_pct) AS disk_used_pct max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_used_pct max(tsdb_num_blocks) AS tsdb_num_blocks values(lane) AS lanes BY _time uni_host cluster
| sort 0 uni_host cluster + _time
| streamstats window=96 global=f current=t sum(eval(if(compaction_failed>0 OR wal_corr>0 OR log_ev>0,1,0))) AS fail_events_24h BY uni_host cluster
| eventstats median(head_series) AS cluster_median_series BY cluster
| eval disk_used_pct=tonumber(coalesce(disk_used_pct,0))
| join type=left max=0 uni_host
    [| inputlookup prometheus_tsdb_inventory.csv
     | eval uni_host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | fields uni_host role retention_days disk_capacity_gb owner_team scheduled_maint_window ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval role=coalesce(role,"unknown")
| eval retention_days=coalesce(retention_days,0)
| eval disk_capacity_gb=coalesce(disk_capacity_gb,0)
| eval sw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(scheduled_maint_window,""))))
| eval maint_hit=if(match(sw,"freeze|change|approved|blackout|maint"),1,0)
| eval fail_signal=if(compaction_failed>0 OR log_ev>0,1,0)
| eval head_bloat=if(isnotnull(head_series) AND isnotnull(cluster_median_series) AND cluster_median_series>500 AND head_series>(2*cluster_median_series),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    fail_signal==1 AND maint_hit==0 AND disk_used_pct>85,"CRITICAL",
    head_bloat==1,"HIGH",
    wal_corr>0,"MEDIUM",
    maint_hit==1,"LOW",
    fail_signal==1 AND disk_used_pct<=85,"HIGH",
    true(),"LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL","free_disk_space_verify_tsdb_path_promtool_tsdb_analyze_restore_snapshot",
    severity=="HIGH" AND head_bloat==1,"reduce_label_cardinality_review_scrape_configs_recording_rules",
    severity=="MEDIUM","inspect_wal_segments_restart_controlled_review_logs_for_corrupt",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_validate_scrape_and_retention_policies")
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(compaction_failed) AS compaction_failed max(wal_corr) AS wal_corr max(head_series) AS head_series max(reload_fail) AS reload_fail max(log_ev) AS log_ev max(disk_used_pct) AS disk_used_pct max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_used_pct max(tsdb_num_blocks) AS tsdb_num_blocks max(fail_events_24h) AS fail_events_24h first(cluster_median_series) AS cluster_median_series first(lanes) AS lanes first(severity) AS severity first(recommended_action) AS recommended_action first(owner_team) AS owner_team first(role) AS role first(retention_days) AS retention_days first(disk_capacity_gb) AS disk_capacity_gb first(scheduled_maint_window) AS scheduled_maint_window BY uni_host cluster
| table uni_host cluster lanes fail_events_24h cluster_median_series compaction_failed wal_corr head_series reload_fail log_ev disk_used_pct mem_used_pct tsdb_num_blocks severity recommended_action owner_team role retention_days disk_capacity_gb scheduled_maint_window last_seen
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used latest(Performance.pctDiskSpaceUsed) AS disk_used_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS tsdb_host
| join type=left max=0 tsdb_host
    [| inputlookup prometheus_tsdb_inventory.csv
     | eval tsdb_host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | fields tsdb_host role retention_days disk_capacity_gb owner_team]
| where cpu_load>0 OR mem_used>0 OR disk_used_pct>0 OR len(role)>0
| table tsdb_host role retention_days disk_capacity_gb owner_team cpu_load mem_used disk_used_pct
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by unified host and cluster with drilldowns to mstats self-metrics, prometheus:log panic lines, prometheus:tsdb JSON head and block stats, Performance disk context, optional linux:disk rows, and joined inventory maintenance metadata; companion line charts for head series and compaction duration.

## Known False Positives

A single logged compaction warning during a graceful `SIGTERM` shutdown can coincide with benign WAL truncation while the head block flushes; treat isolated lines as noise unless `prometheus_tsdb_compactions_failed_total` increases across multiple scrapes or TSDB status shows persistent head growth. Operators who intentionally delete blocks using `promtool tsdb` repair workflows during forensic exercises will generate scary log fragments that match this search even when the cluster is healthy post-restoration; require inventory change tickets and maint_window metadata before paging. After tightening `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` or `--storage.tsdb.retention.size`, compaction duration histograms often spike for several hours while the engine rewrites overlapping ranges; that burst is expected capacity work rather than a fault if free space remains above policy and failure counters stay flat. Head series counts routinely jump during large scrape config rollouts that add many new targets; compare against a seven-day baseline per job label set rather than absolute thresholds alone. WAL truncation events logged shortly after retention-size edits can look like corruption until sequence numbers stabilize; pair log text with `prometheus_tsdb_wal_corruptions_total` and reload failure metrics. Snapshot tools and backup agents that hold file descriptors open across compaction windows may stall merges briefly; correlate with vendor snapshot schedules before declaring engine damage. Kernel page cache pressure on busy nodes can lengthen `prometheus_tsdb_compaction_duration_seconds` without raising failure counters; compare against `linux:disk` queue depth and host steal time. WAL corruption counters sometimes reflect recoverable segments that Prometheus replays after a clean stop, whereas true block corruption often implies missing chunks and requires `promtool tsdb analyze` plus restore-from-object-store procedures; do not conflate the two in executive summaries. Thanos sidecars uploading partially compacted blocks during store-gateway cutovers can create transient TSDB status skew between leaf and global views; validate object-store consistency before muting. During HA failover events where `remote_write` endpoints are repointed, backlog gauges can mask local compaction health; examine leaf self-metrics independently of remote queue depth. False alarms may cluster when a passive replica is promoted and scrapes duplicate self-metrics until service discovery settles; dedupe by `instance` label and enforce maint_window matches from `prometheus_tsdb_inventory.csv`.
